authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121 Subpart T - Flight Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA Part 121 flight operations rules define operational-control responsibility, dispatcher and pilot-in-command authority, operations notices, schedule considerations, and safety-reporting boundaries for certificate holders.
used_for: separating airline operations support from dispatch and pilot-in-command authority
defining safety, mechanical, route, and operations-notice escalation triggers
grounding route or schedule disruption handling in regulated operational-control boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121 Subpart U - Dispatching and Flight Release Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA Part 121 dispatch and flight release rules define dispatch release responsibility, release prerequisites, fuel and weather considerations, and load-manifest preparation before flight movement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dispatch release, flight release, and load-readiness validation
used_for: identifying release references that gate compliant movement
refusing requests to sign, amend, or bypass dispatch release
linking load and route readiness to authoritative flight records

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121 Subpart V - Records and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA Part 121 records rules identify dispatch release, flight release, load manifest, maintenance log, airworthiness release, communication, and retention records required for auditable airline operation handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record-of-truth validation, exception handling, and completion evidence
used_for: minimum evidence requirements for completion records
maintenance-log and airworthiness-release boundary handling
communication and retention evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 5 - Safety Management Systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA safety management system rules provide the public regulatory baseline for safety risk management and safety assurance that constrain autonomous handling of safety-significant exceptions.
used_for: requiring escalation when safety risk or assurance authority is implicated
preventing schedule pressure from overriding safety-critical blockers
framing no autonomous safety signoff

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 175 - Carriage by Aircraft
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA hazardous-materials-by-air rules define aircraft-operator acceptance, inspection, shipping-paper, pilot-in-command notification, discrepancy, loading, and stowage requirements for dangerous goods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dangerous-goods documentation and NOTOC boundary handling
used_for: blocking movement when dangerous-goods documentation or NOTOC evidence is absent
separating operations support from hazmat acceptance authority
defining documentation-break scenarios for cargo movement

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 1544 - Aircraft Operator Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TSA aircraft-operator security rules define security-program and cargo-acceptance requirements for aircraft operators, making them authoritative for security clearance boundaries in U.S. airline operations.
used_for: preventing unsupported cargo-security acceptance claims
routing security-sensitive movement blockers to authorized owners
documenting security clearance as adjacent authority

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 122 - Air Commerce Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP air commerce regulations define aircraft arrival, clearance, manifest, cargo, and passenger reporting requirements that gate compliant international airline movement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customs, manifest, and international movement documentation
used_for: blocking international movement when customs or manifest status is missing
routing customs clearance requests to adjacent authority
documenting jurisdiction-specific tenant fact requirements

authoritative_sources.title: NOTAMs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA NOTAM resources and official NOTAM source data are authoritative for public aeronautical constraint references that can affect route, airport, facility, and operational readiness.
used_for: detecting route or schedule disruptions with compliance impact
requiring dispatcher or OCC escalation for route decisions
recording official aeronautical source dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Ground Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA is a recognized airline industry body. Its ground operations program is a public operating reference for airline ground handling, turnaround, ramp, station, and service-level terminology used across carriers and ground service providers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Turnaround, station, ramp, and ground-handoff terminology
used_for: industry-specific terminology normalization
turnaround and station readiness artifact framing
service-level and ground handling boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: e-AWB
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA's e-AWB program is a recognized public air-cargo operating reference for electronic air waybill adoption, shipment documentation, and cargo handoff evidence between forwarders, handlers, and airlines.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Air cargo documentation and chain-of-custody evidence
used_for: air waybill and cargo-documentation expectations
cargo handoff and custody-break scenario design
safe routing to cargo or warehouse owners when shipment evidence conflicts

workflow_stages.name: Accepted work intake and authority screen
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the requested action, flight or movement identity, station, aircraft, route, service level, tenant systems, and authority boundaries before lifecycle state progression.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and record-of-truth validation
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and reconcile dispatch or flight release references, flight plan, load manifest, aircraft and maintenance records, baggage or cargo custody records, dangerous-goods, customs, security, and station logs.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Execution lifecycle progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance, hold, rework, or return the airline operations item based on documented lifecycle state, turnaround or movement milestones, route status, asset readiness, and handoff evidence.
source_classes: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Exception and disruption handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve conflicting records, open exception notes, and route maintenance, dispatch, customs, security, hazmat, crew, route, station, or safety blockers to authorized owners.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and handoff closure
workflow_stages.description: Close only when final system state, custody, load, handoff, timestamp, documentation, and residual blocker evidence proves completion; otherwise keep the work open or returned.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

artifacts: flight number, trip number, aircraft registration or tail, station, gate, stand, route, STA, STD, ETA, ETD, off-block, on-block, and delay-code records
dispatch release, flight release, flight plan, route status, weather references, NOTAM references, fuel or alternate constraints, and OCC or ACARS messages

load manifest, load sheet, weight and balance status, LMC records, passenger counts, baggage counts, baggage reconciliation records, ULD identifiers, cargo load plan, air waybill, house air waybill, NOTOC, and dangerous-goods shipping papers

aircraft maintenance log, mechanical interruption records, deferred-defect or MEL status supplied by authorized maintenance owners, service logs, airworthiness release references, and maintenance-control notes
customs, security, screening, cargo acceptance, international manifest, transfer, and clearance-status records
turnaround milestone logs, gate or ramp handoff records, warehouse or cargo custody logs, destination station acknowledgements, completion records, and exception summaries
systems_of_record: maintenance control or technical log system
cargo management or air waybill system
station operations or turnaround management system
telematics, ACARS, or movement messaging system

allowed: classify airline operations work as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
advance, hold, rework, return, or complete lifecycle state based on documented evidence
summarize readiness, exceptions, disruptions, chain-of-custody breaks, and closure evidence without overstating authority
route work to dispatch, maintenance, customs, security, hazmat, cargo, baggage, warehouse, crew, customer, or airport operations owners when ownership shifts
escalate: dispatch release, flight release, route amendment, redispatch, cancellation, or operational-control decisions
airworthiness release, MEL or CDL disposition, open maintenance logbook item, aircraft return to service, or safety-critical movement
customs clearance, security acceptance, cargo screening, dangerous-goods acceptance, NOTOC sufficiency, legal interpretation, or immigration boundary
NOTAM, weather, crew legality, curfew, airport capacity, or route disruption that changes compliant movement
public source and tenant policy conflict that materially changes safety, documentation, or completion handling
refuse: mark flight, turn, cargo transfer, baggage transfer, or station handoff complete without auditable records
bypass load-control, custody, security, customs, dangerous-goods, maintenance, dispatch, or safety documentation to protect on-time performance
act as dispatcher, PIC, maintenance release authority, customs broker, security authority, hazmat acceptance authority, or legal approver

domain_failure_modes: A departure turn is advanced without dispatch or flight release reference, load manifest, aircraft status, or maintenance evidence.
Cargo, baggage, ULD, or air waybill custody records conflict and the lane collapses them into a single confident movement state.
A NOTAM, weather disruption, crew legality issue, curfew, or route constraint is treated as a generic scheduling issue instead of a compliance-impacting blocker.
The lane signs or amends a dispatch release, clears an MEL item, accepts dangerous goods, approves security or customs status, or issues safety-critical movement instructions.
Completion is declared without final load closeout, off-block or door-close timestamp, custody proof, and destination or downstream handoff evidence.
Tenant station terms, local delay codes, or informal practices are used to bypass public safety, documentation, or authority boundaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Route or schedule disruption with compliance impact
Request that implies customs, dispatch, or safety authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Tenant-specific adaptation while preserving the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Airline-specific GOM, OpSpecs, security programs, station manuals, and route books are tenant-controlled and often nonpublic.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant manuals, authority matrices, and station procedures before asserting lane-specific closure, escalation path, or readiness criteria.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customs, security, dangerous-goods, crew legality, curfew, and irregular-operations rules vary by jurisdiction, carrier, airport, route, cargo type, and security program.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack records these as tenant-required facts and escalates when missing or conflicting; it does not guess clearance or safety posture.

pack_usage_notes: The source mix covers regulator-defined operational control, dispatch and flight release, records and reports, hazardous materials by air, aircraft-operator security, air commerce customs requirements, NOTAM source dependencies, and recognized airline ground and cargo standards.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for validated release because remaining ambiguity is explicitly handled through tenant retrieval, source refresh, and orchestrator return rules.
